mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a backup and restore operator for Kubernetes that allows you to back up cluster resources and persistent volumes, facilitating migration and disaster recovery. It is particularly useful for managing backups in production environments and can be run on various cloud platforms or on-premises. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration of storage backends and potential complexity in managing backup schedules.

steps.title: Add the Velero Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This repository contains the Velero Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Velero with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Velero in the specified namespace with the necessary configuration for your cloud provider.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

```

> Replace 'my-velero-bucket' with your actual S3 bucket name.
commands: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

steps.title: Verify Velero installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Velero deployment is running correctly in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> You should see the Velero server pod running.

steps.title: Create a backup
steps.description:
This command creates a backup of your Kubernetes resources and persistent volumes.

```bash

velero backup create my-backup --include-namespaces my-namespace

```

> Replace 'my-namespace' with the namespace you want to back up.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This command removes Velero from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all backups and associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

```

> This will remove the Velero deployment and all related resources.

uninstall.title: Delete the Velero namespace
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Velero namespace, which may contain orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

> This will remove the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current Velero configuration and backups. This step will upgrade Velero to the latest version.

```bash

velero backup get

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --version 1.18.0

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> Check existing backups before proceeding.
> This command upgrades Velero to the specified version.
> Verify that the Velero pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup: the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the specified namespace does not exist or if there are issues with the Velero configuration.

**Fix:**
Ensure the namespace exists and check the Velero configuration for correctness.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Backup storage location not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified backup storage location is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the backup storage location configuration in the Velero installation command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to restore backup: no matching resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the resources in the backup do not match the current cluster state.

**Fix:**
Check the backup contents using 'velero backup describe my-backup' and ensure the resources exist.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a connectivity issue with the Kubernetes API server.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure that your Kubernetes cluster is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for additional cloud providers, improved backup performance, and enhanced CLI commands.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old backup and restore APIs in favor of new versions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17, ensure to review the new configuration options for cloud providers.

resolution.summary: You should see the Velero server pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n velero'.
codeSnippets: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
